Materials EngineeringAjay Gautam

### **Ajay Gautam**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Ajay Gautam is an Assistant Professor in Materials Engineering at IIT Jammu. His research focuses on the structure–transport relationships in materials for lithium-ion and solid-state batteries, advanced materials processing, and characterization using X-ray and neutron diffraction. Previously, he was an R&D Scientist at Samsung SDI Europe GmbH, Germany, where he focused on the processing of battery materials for next-generation battery technologies. Dr. Gautam earned his Ph.D. at the University of Münster and Justus Liebig University Giessen, Germany, under the supervision of Prof. Wolfgang Zeier. His doctoral work established key parameters of structural disorder and ion kinetics to unravel the structure–ion transport correlation in solid electrolytes. He pursued postdoctoral research

Materials EngineeringSarita

### **Sarita**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Sarita works in the field of computational and experimental materials research, with a primary focus on phase transformations and microstructural evolution in different alloy systems. Her research combines density functional theory (DFT), atom probe tomography (APT), and small-angle scattering techniques (SAXS/SANS) to understand structure–property relationships at the atomic and nanoscale levels. She has extensive research experience in structural and nuclear materials, particularly in understanding microstructural evolution under extreme conditions such as high temperatures and radiation exposure. She also has a strong interest in the design of rare-earth-free magnetic materials, where microstructural tuning can be utilized to achieve desired magnetic properties.

The famous quote by Richard Feynman "there is plenty of room at the bottom", is also apt for Materials Engineers, who strive to engineer the atomic-level structure of materials, in order to obtain desired properties.
